filter.scope: Balanced PTD: power transmission, grid, data center power, AI infrastructure, renewables, HV equipment, GIS, energy infrastructure

ai.summary: Based on the available description, Cami’s laundromat generated roughly $475,000 in revenue in 2024, plus nearly $30,000 in rent from an adjacent salon. Documents reviewed by CNBC Make It show about $119,000 in profits (some reinvested) and $66,000 paid to Cami as salary.
bullets: Additional rental income: ~ $30,000 from neighboring salon
Reported profit: ~ $119,000; portion reinvested into the business
Source: documents reviewed by CNBC Make It (no transcript available)
takeaways: Ancillary rental income can meaningfully increase total cash flow from a small commercial site—important to include in revenue assessments during site evaluations.
Reported profit and owner compensation demonstrate laundromats can generate steady operating cash flows relevant for underwriting commercial loads or service contracts.
Reinvestment of profits signals ongoing capital needs; factor documented reinvestment when assessing asset lifecycle, maintenance budgets, or retrofit opportunities.
When evaluating small commercial host sites for projects or partnerships, prioritize documented financials (revenues, ancillary income, profits, owner pay) as part of due diligence.
